title: Iran's war within a war may be about to begin as Kurdish troops gather along border | ABC NEWS
description: The mountain ranges along the northern border of Iran shelter the Peshmerga, the men and women at the heart of the other war for the country.  Many are Kurds, an ethnic minority, who are ready to strike back against the Iranian regime.  Video by Karishma Vyas. Social version by David Douglas Stuart for the ABC News Social Media Team. description: Since the outbreak of a new war in the Middle East, the Australian share market has plummeted by more than 6 per cent. title: UN expert calls out nations for not condemning US - Israel justifications for war | ABC NEWS
description: "It's a recipe for further destabilisation, possible insurgency, fragmentation politically within Iran in the region, and destabilisation of neighbours through refugee flows." — Ben Saul, UN Special Rapporteur on the promotion and protection of human rights and fundamental freedoms while countering terrorism.  The war that began with a US-Israeli bombing campaign at the end of February has so far killed about 2,000 people, including Iran's supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, and damaged much of Iran's military and security apparatus.  US and Israeli officials say the aim is to destroy Iran's missile and nuclear programmes, but Trump has also demanded Iran's "unconditional surrender" and the power to determine its leaders.  Iran has responded in kind, throwing global energy markets and transport into chaos and spreading the conflict across the Middle East, while the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps has tightened its grip on power and threatened to crush any unrest.  Israeli officials have said that one of their objectives is to weaken the security apparatus so that Iran's people can take control of their own destiny.  Reporting by Reuters. title: In 1983, Iran threatened to close the Strait of Hormuz | ABC NEWS
description: In 1983, the Strait of Hormuz became a major flashpoint in the Iran-Iraq War after Tehran threatened to close the waterway and disrupt global oil supply if Iraq attacked Iranian oil installations with its new French bombers.   Despite the threats, Iran didn't close the strait back then.  ABC NEWS provides around the clock coverage of news events as they break in Australia and abroad.
